mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not statistically higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.2 percent within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.2 percent, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where inventory drawdowns outpace spending, leading to margin compression and negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns when INDPRO falls more than 1 percent and RSAFS rises more than 2 percent month-over-month, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, subsequent increases in core inflation directly reduce real equity valuations as discount rates remain elevated, depressing forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL rises in periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal flips to positive predictive power in the same regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP directly expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as cash flow projections are revised upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or earnings revisions fail to track GDP surprises over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals a sharp cooling in construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering risk-off positioning that depresses equity prices over the next week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly negative following monthly drops in HOUST exceeding 5 percent, or the signal produces false positives in more than 60 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and persistent inflation creates stagflationary pressure, forcing the Fed to prioritize price stability over employment support, which compresses equity multiples over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both UNRATE and CPIAUCSL rise simultaneously, or the interaction term explains less than 2 percent of forward return variance.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-capacity utilization environment, a sustained rise in industrial production signals supply chain bottlenecks and input cost inflation, which erodes corporate margins and depresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline when INDPRO rises above 102, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2010 data.
